title: Enemion savilei
intro: Enemion savilei is a type of perennial plant in the lily family (Liliaceae), native to China. Traditionally, Enemion savilei is used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Enemion savilei include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Enemion savilei is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Enemion savilei is classified as a member of the family Liliaceae, although some sources place it in the family Colchicaceae. It belongs to the genus Enemion, which is a small group of flowering plants native to North America. The Enemion genus is further divided into two species, with Enemion savilei being one of them. This plant is typically characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and white or purple flowers. Within the Enemion genus, Enemion savilei is often distinguished from its closest relative, Enemion biternatum.
